question: A process has a message start event named `OrderReceived`. Three new messages named `OrderReceived` are published, each with a different payload. How many process instances are created?

options.text: Three — one new instance per new message.
One — message start events de-duplicate publishes by message name.
Zero — message start events still require an explicit `CreateProcessInstance` call.
Three only if every message also carries a correlation key value.

a.text: The docs state that for a process with a message start event, a new instance is created for each new message.

b.text: There is no de-duplication by message name for distinct new messages; each new message creates an instance.

c.text: Process instances are created implicitly by message and timer start events; an explicit `CreateProcessInstance` call is not required.

d.text: Message start events do not require a correlation key to fire — the docs do not condition instance creation on correlation key presence for new messages.

explanation: Process instances are created implicitly by start events. Message start events react to each new published message of the matching name by creating a new instance, alongside the explicit `CreateProcessInstance` and `CreateProcessInstanceWithResult` commands.
